column6: V in decimal mode is undefined; ares/bsnes/Mesen2 share a convention, which is agreement, not authority

column6: WDC/GTE/VLSI instruction-operation tables agree on these rows; docs/accuracysnes-timing-oracle.md
WDC note (17) asserts RWB is low during the modify cycle in emulation mode; the GTE and VLSI renderings of the same table are silent

WDC 65C816 datasheet, vector table; SNESdev Wiki vectors
WDC datasheet: XCE exchanges carry and E, nothing else
WDC datasheet: the block-move indices are bank offsets
WDC datasheet: m/x are forced while E=1, whatever writes P
SNESdev Errata, 65C816 section; anomie's addressing notes
upstream marks the relative addressing modes r/rl "XXX: untested"; no source vouches for the bank-boundary case
WDC datasheet: E=1 forces x=1, so the indices are 8-bit
WDC datasheet: WAI wakes on the interrupt line; I gates the vector
WDC datasheet: MVN rewinds PC by 3 per iteration, so RTI re-enters
the stated 1-cycle latency is 1.5 dots, below what a cart can resolve; recorded in buckets rather than asserted
WDC datasheet: the vector is 16-bit, so the handler runs in bank 0
WDC datasheet: RTI pulls PBR in native mode only
superfamicom.org describes the wrap as happening "theoretically"; the dossier records the row as UNVERIFIED, so it is observed rather than asserted
SNESdev Wiki, PPU registers; fullsnes; anomie
the bsnes/ares lineage and Mesen2 model two distinct latches
the bit reports a board wiring input, so no software-visible value is correct or incorrect; recorded rather than asserted
fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki: PPU1's write-twice registers share one byte latch, so $210D/$210E and $211B-$211E interfere with each other

fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki agree the Mode 7 multiplier is the renderer's and is busy during active display, but neither states which intermediate it holds at a given moment, and that is the only thing a cart can observe
fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki: sprite range evaluation selects on Y alone, so a              sprite at X = $100 occupies a range slot despite being entirely off-screen

fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki agree the renderer drives the OAM address during active display, but neither states which byte evaluation has reached at a given moment, and the cores split: Mesen2 models it, RustySNES and snes9x return the programmed address
SNESdev Wiki, PPU registers; fullsnes; anomie

RustySNES and snes9x re-close the VRAM window on a mid-frame overscan enable and Mesen2 does not, so the references split one each way; the dossier's repro cannot break the tie because it read-modify-writes the write-only $2133

SNESdev Wiki, PPU registers: return latch, refill latch, then increment, with VMAIN bit 7 selecting which of $2139/$213A triggers it; bsnes and ares both refill before the step in sfc/ppu/io.cpp, and snes9x and Mesen2 agree

superfamicom.org registers: $2137 latches the H/V counter only if $4201 bit 7 is set, and no latching can occur while it is 0; snes9x and Mesen2 both gate it. What the read returns is a separate question, split out into C3.11

the sources say only that $2137 reads back as open bus; snes9x and RustySNES present PPU1's latch while Mesen2 presents the CPU's, and nothing available decides between two physically reasonable readings
SNESdev Wiki, Memory map / timing; fullsnes
SNESdev Wiki, Memory map / timing; fullsnes

fullsnes' PPU H-Counter-Latch Quantities histogram, a direct hardware measurement: dots 323 and 327 latch six times, dot 340 never. bsnes, ares and Mesen2 all implement it; snes9x uses 322/326 and is the outlier
no source pins the fired dot at single-dot precision; recorded as the before/after guard for T-06-A's clock-domain comparator change
SNESdev Errata states overlapping $4203/$4206 operation is undefined
anomie regs.txt r1157 and nocash fullsnes, independently; implemented by bsnes/ares/Mesen2. No known hardware test ROM
SNES Development Manual Bk I 21.1; SNESdev Wiki; fullsnes
SNES Development Manual Bk I 21.1 (DMA at 2.68MHz regardless of address)
the dossier conditions the extra line on $213F.7 (the field), which this test does not control — sampling V across an uncontrolled field cannot assert a line count
SNESdev Wiki, Timing; fullsnes — the sub-cycle poll point is not CPU-observable, so its consequence is measured instead
SNESdev PPU registers places the 50/60Hz bit at bit 3, which overlaps the PPU2 version field; fullsnes places it at bit 4
the $4211 poll loop is coarser than the dot the comparator fires on, so the exact H position is not resolvable from software by polling

fullsnes and anomie put the pause at 40 clocks near line-clock 536, but ares' own source calls its refresh pattern technically wrong and only right on average, and docs/accuracy-ledger.md scopes refresh out of RustySNES on the measurement that its frame length is already correct without one
fullsnes $4207-$420A: HTIME is 0-339 and VTIME 0-261 (NTSC) / 0-311 (PAL), both held in nine bits

superfamicom.org's timing page states the exception and gives no mechanism; its timing text derives from fullsnes, so the two are one source, and no test ROM verifies it. ares, bsnes, Mesen2 and snes9x were each searched and none implements it
ares and bsnes both model the latch and serialize it
fullsnes: "does not cause a write to occur"
SNESdev Wiki, HDMA; anomie's timing doc; fullsnes

fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki record that enabling HDMA outside vblank produces erroneous writes from uninitialised A2An/NLTRn, but what those writes contain is a function of the previous frame's leftover state and is specified nowhere
fullsnes: $2180->WRAM writes, but the value written is invalid
fullsnes register table and the SNESdev DMA-registers page agree independently; ares and bsnes default every channel field to match
the errata names the ranges invalid but does not specify what is read instead; the substitute is open bus, whose content is core-specific and time-dependent
the dossier records a repeating 32x$00 / 32x$FF fill and marks it chip-dependent and informational; RustySNES, snes9x and Mesen2 all boot APU RAM uniformly zero instead
SNESdev Wiki, SPC700 reference; fullsnes — flagged as errata
SNESdev Wiki SPC700 reference and fullsnes, both flagging DIV as valid only for quotients up to 511; the values past it follow E1.03's overflow formula

SNESdev Wiki SPC700 timers and fullsnes: a 0->1 on a $F1 timer-enable bit resets that timer's stage-2 divider and stage-3 output counter
SNESdev Wiki, SPC700; fullsnes — flagged as errata
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: of the four gaussian additions the first cannot overflow, the second wraps in 16 bits and the third saturates
the dossier names four nibble patterns and asks what a decoder makes of each without stating an expected value for any of them; the row's content is the measurement
fullsnes and anomie: the SPC700's 1.024 MHz clock and the DSP's 32 kHz output rate fix the ratio at 32 CPU cycles per output sample

both sources agree FLG bit 7 makes the DSP behave as $E0 and force every voice into release, and contradict each other on what ENDX then reads: nocash says $FF, anomie says 0. The dossier marks the row [CONFLICT] and asks for a golden vector
the SNESdev Wiki SPC700 reference and fullsnes both give XCN as 5 cycles, against 2 for NOP
SNESdev Wiki SPC700 reference and fullsnes: DBNZ dp,rel reads its operand, decrements it and writes it back, and $FD-$FF are read-to-clear
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the noise generator's step rate is set by FLG bits 0-4, and a voice's pitch register does not participate
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the noise shift register resets to $4000, with taps bit0 XOR bit1 feeding bit 14

fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the BRR header is ssssffle, shift in bits 7-4, and a nibble decodes as (nibble << shift) >> 1
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: key-off begins the release ramp and does not halt BRR decoding, which continues to follow loop points and set ENDX

fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the per-voice mix clamps to 16 bits after each addition rather than accumulating and clamping once
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the echo buffer holds four bytes per sample, a 16-bit left sample followed by a 16-bit right one
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the echo FIR keeps a separate eight-sample history and accumulator per channel; only the coefficients are shared

SNESdev Wiki, S-DSP envelopes; fullsnes; anomie's DSP doc
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: attack rate $F fires every sample with a step of +1024, rather than the +32 on a counter tick every other rate uses
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: release steps -8 per sample regardless of ADSR, which is why a custom release has to be built from GAIN
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the decay phase indexes the counter table at d*2+16, stepping E -= 1 then E -= E>>8
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the sustain phase indexes the counter table with the ADSR2 rate field directly, unlike decay's d*2+16
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the decay phase ends when (E >> 8) equals the ADSR2 sustain level, giving a boundary of $100 per level
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: the attack phase indexes the counter table at a*2+1 and steps the envelope by +32
the dossier records the GAIN-sourced boundary as [ERRATA]; RustySNES takes it from ADSR2 instead, which E7.07 asserts, so which behaviour is right is not settled here
SNESdev Wiki, S-DSP envelopes; fullsnes; anomie's DSP doc
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: KON is held for five output samples while the directory and the first BRR block are fetched, before the envelope starts
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: KON re-enters the key-on sequence unconditionally, resetting the BRR pointer and zeroing the envelope, whether or not the voice was already playing

KON/KOFF are sampled every second output sample, so whether a short pulse is seen depends on where the poll falls inside it -- which makes the outcome phase-dependent rather than fixed, the same hedge E8.05 and E8.06 carry as "usually"
fullsnes and anomie's DSP doc: KON zeroes the envelope before its attack, and KOFF outranks KON, so the pair silences immediately where KOFF alone ramps

fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki controller protocol: the shift register presents B, Y, Select, Start, Up, Down, Left, Right, A, X, L, R and then four zero bits
RustySNES, snes9x and Mesen2 all return $41 for the absolute read and $01 for the long one -- identical bytes, so bits 7-2 follow the CPU bus in all three
fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki: bit 0 of $4200 arms the automatic joypad read, and with it clear $4218-$421F are not written
fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki: bits 3-0 of the auto-read result identify the device, and a standard controller reports 0000
fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki: the auto-read result holds the sixteen shifted bits in clock order, most significant first, so B is bit 15
fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki: while $4016 bit 0 is high the shift registers reload continuously rather than shifting, so an automatic read taken across it returns the same bit in every position

F1.12 says results are valid by V = $E3, which does not reconcile with F1.09's 4224-cycle duration and F1.08's start window; no source says which to believe, and the cores split on whether the result appears at once or progressively
fullsnes: RDIO reads the WRIO output pins, which are open-collector, so with nothing driving them low the value read is the value written

fullsnes and the SNESdev Wiki power-on table: $4201 = $FF, HTIME and VTIME = $1FF; the other registers in the row are observed by B5.05 and B1.01
the dossier marks the whole row [UNDEFINED] and says to report it and never assert it; half the registers it names are write-only and cannot be reported at all
the dossier marks the row [UNDEFINED] and asks for a golden vector by name: no canonical WRAM fill exists and real consoles disagree
